visa_types.name: Tourist
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kwt/en/aut/travel-insurance)
Short  stay visas (Schengen)
-Visa requirements for Kuwaiti.
-Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti
-Visa requirements for Kuwaiti. (Arabic)
-Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti (Arabic)

The short stay visa application must be submitted on a duly completed application form, which can be downloaded free of charge from this web page or can also be obtained free of charge from Austria Diplomatic Missions or Consular Offices abroad.
When submitting the visa application, the established fee must be paid (generally). This fee is non-refundable and therefore will not be refunded if the visa application is unsuccessful. Under certain circumstances applicants may be entitled to a reduction or may be exempt from paying the fee. Please consult the Diplomatic Missions or Austria Consular Offices. For all other necessary requirements, please consult the Diplomatic
Mission or Consular Office where the visa application is being made as these may vary according to the reason for the visit and the applicant's country of origin.

The period for processing short stay (Schengen) visa applications is minimum fifteen calendar days from the date the application is submitted. This period can be extended to a maximum of 30 calendar days in specific cases, especially if a more detailed examination of the application is required. By way of exception, in specific cases in which additional documentation is required, this period can be extended to a maximum of 60 calendar days.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:Tourist]
Please  Note:
 All payments will be collected in local currency as per the local exchange rate. You may refer to the current exchange rate to arrive at an approximate amount. 
The Visa fees can be paid by Cash and Debitcard. It is  subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
The following categories of short stay applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
 Children below 6 years
 Family members of EEA member states
 All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.forms:
Visa requirements for Kuwaiti.
Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti
Application form in both German & English language 
Application form in both German & Arabic language 
Letter of Guarantee for Housemaid
Parents’ Consent letter for Minors
Private Invitation- basic requirements.
- Since 01.06.09 it has become mandatory for the inviter to produce an EVE (=Electronic Guarantee letter) with the respective Austrian alien police authority! This new procedure is substituting for the former "Paper-Guarantee letter" which was attested by an Austrian notary public. Once the EVE has been done in Austria by the inviter he/she will be given a CODE (e.g. AUH090000001). This code shall be forwarded to the visa applicant who will produce it to the consulate once applying for the visa. The consulate will be able to obtain all details given in Austria by the inviter to the alien police. See
http://www.bmi.gv.at/cms/BMI_Fremdenpolizei/einreise_visa/Visum_6.aspx (http://www.bmi.gv.at/cms/BMI_Fremdenpolizei/einreise_visa/Visum_6.aspx)
- Passport copy of the inviting person in Austria.
- Letter in original from employer and/or sponsor (non-objection letter) stating designation, date of employment, salary, period of stay, stamped and signed (name should also be printed).
- Company trade license (if applicant is the owner or the partner); original and one copy.
- Personal bank statements listing transactions for the last three months.
- A link to be established between the invitee in Austria and the visa applicant in Kuwait (a letter to be enclosed from the inviting party in Austria explaining the relationship).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Please note applications can only be submitted maximum 6 months prior to the intended travel dates.
- Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
- Only Kuwaiti nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Application Center in Kuwait.
- Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. The Embassy of Austria in Kuwait reserves the right to invite the applicant to a personal interview.
- Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted and the visa application can only be processed upon receipt of ALL documents required by the embassy (see List of required documents below).
- Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
- Applications for visas have to be received by The Embassy of Austria in Kuwait at least THREE WEEKS prior to the beginning of the journey as approval takes up to 15 days.
- Please be reminded that processing period depends on the Embassy. If further assessment is required, the processing period could take longer than the above mentioned period of time. So please ensure that you have sufficient time to submit the visa application.
- Please note, Austria visa application center cannot accept visa application submitted less than15 days of intend travel date.
- If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- Please note applications can only be submitted maximum 6 months prior to the intended travel dates.
- Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
- Only Kuwaiti nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Application Center in Kuwait.
- Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. The Embassy of Austria in Kuwait reserves the right to invite the applicant to a personal interview.
- Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted and the visa application can only be processed upon receipt of ALL documents required by the embassy (see List of required documents below).
- Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
- Applications for visas have to be received by The Embassy of Austria in Kuwait at least THREE WEEKS prior to the beginning of the journey as approval takes up to 15 days.
- Please be reminded that processing period depends on the Embassy. If further assessment is required, the processing period could take longer than the above mentioned period of time. So please ensure that you have sufficient time to submit the visa application.
- Please note, Austria visa application center cannot accept visa application submitted less than15 days of intend travel date.
- If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:

## Visa Fees

[Table:Tourist]
Please  Note:
 All payments will be collected in local currency as per the local exchange rate. You may refer to the current exchange rate to arrive at an approximate amount. 
The Visa fees can be paid by Cash and Debitcard. It is  subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
The following categories of short stay applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
 Children below 6 years
 Family members of EEA member states
 All fees are non-refundable. 

## Documents Required
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kwt/en/aut/travel-insurance)
Short  stay visas (Schengen)
-Visa requirements for Kuwaiti.
-Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti
-Visa requirements for Kuwaiti. (Arabic)
-Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti (Arabic)

The short stay visa application must be submitted on a duly completed application form, which can be downloaded free of charge from this web page or can also be obtained free of charge from Austria Diplomatic Missions or Consular Offices abroad.
When submitting the visa application, the established fee must be paid (generally). This fee is non-refundable and therefore will not be refunded if the visa application is unsuccessful. Under certain circumstances applicants may be entitled to a reduction or may be exempt from paying the fee. Please consult the Diplomatic Missions or Austria Consular Offices. For all other necessary requirements, please consult the Diplomatic
Mission or Consular Office where the visa application is being made as these may vary according to the reason for the visit and the applicant's country of origin.

The period for processing short stay (Schengen) visa applications is minimum fifteen calendar days from the date the application is submitted. This period can be extended to a maximum of 30 calendar days in specific cases, especially if a more detailed examination of the application is required. By way of exception, in specific cases in which additional documentation is required, this period can be extended to a maximum of 60 calendar days. 

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in color and:
                                                                          
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling (without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.Photo booth meeting these requirements is available at the centre. 

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
Visa requirements for Kuwaiti.
Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti
Application form in both German & English language 
Application form in both German & Arabic language 
Letter of Guarantee for Housemaid
Parents’ Consent letter for Minors
Private Invitation- basic requirements.
- Since 01.06.09 it has become mandatory for the inviter to produce an EVE (=Electronic Guarantee letter) with the respective Austrian alien police authority! This new procedure is substituting for the former "Paper-Guarantee letter" which was attested by an Austrian notary public. Once the EVE has been done in Austria by the inviter he/she will be given a CODE (e.g. AUH090000001). This code shall be forwarded to the visa applicant who will produce it to the consulate once applying for the visa. The consulate will be able to obtain all details given in Austria by the inviter to the alien police. See
http://www.bmi.gv.at/cms/BMI_Fremdenpolizei/einreise_visa/Visum_6.aspx (http://www.bmi.gv.at/cms/BMI_Fremdenpolizei/einreise_visa/Visum_6.aspx)
- Passport copy of the inviting person in Austria.
- Letter in original from employer and/or sponsor (non-objection letter) stating designation, date of employment, salary, period of stay, stamped and signed (name should also be printed).
- Company trade license (if applicant is the owner or the partner); original and one copy.
- Personal bank statements listing transactions for the last three months.
- A link to be established between the invitee in Austria and the visa applicant in Kuwait (a letter to be enclosed from the inviting party in Austria explaining the relationship).

## To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list 
- Save the form on your computer.

 Please note that all documents mentioned in the list are absolutely necessary for a Schengen visa. Please bring ALL the documents to the Embassy as incomplete applications cannot be processed in time and might affect your travel plans.
The short stay visa application must be submitted on a duly completed application form, which can be downloaded free of charge from this web page or can also be obtained free of charge from Austria Diplomatic Missions or Consular Offices abroad.
 When submitting the visa application, the established fee must be paid (generally). This fee is non-refundable and therefore will not be refunded if the visa application is unsuccessful. Under certain circumstances applicants may be entitled to a reduction or may be exempt from paying the fee. Please consult the Diplomatic Missions or Austria Consular Offices. For all other necessary requirements, please consult the Diplomatic Mission or Consular Office where the visa application is being made as these may vary according to the reason for the visit and the applicant's country of origin.
The maximum period for processing short stay (Schengen) visa applications is fifteen calendar days from the date the application is submitted. This period can be extended to a maximum of 30 calendar days in specific cases, especially if a more detailed examination of the application is required. By way of exception, in specific cases in which additional documentation is required, this period can be extended to a maximum of 60 calendar days. 
If the visa is issued, it must be collected in person from the competent Diplomatic Mission or Consular Office, or collected by a duly authorized representative, within a period of one month from the date that notification is given of the award. 
In addition: If traveling on business:

visa_types.forms:
Austria Visa  Application form in both German & English language 
Austria Visa   Application form in both German & Arabic language 
-Visa requirements for Kuwaiti.
-Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti
- Electronic Invitation letter from Austria:
See - http://www.bmi.gv.at/cms/BMI_Fremdenpolizei/einreise_visa/Visum_6.aspx (http://www.bmi.gv.at/cms/BMI_Fremdenpolizei/einreise_visa/Visum_6.aspx)
- Original Letter from employer and/or sponsor stating designation, date of employment, salary, period of stay, purpose of visit, stating full responsibility for employee’s

- Company trade license (if applicant is the owner or the partner); original and one copy.
- Hotel reservation for the whole duration of stay.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Please note applications can only be submitted maximum 6 months prior to the intended travel dates.
- Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
- Only Kuwaiti nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Application Center in Kuwait.
- Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. The Embassy of Austria in Kuwait reserves the right to invite the applicant to a personal interview.
- Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted and the visa application can only be processed upon receipt of ALL documents required by the embassy (see List of  required documents below).
- Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
- Applications for visas have to be received by The Embassy of Austria in Kuwait at least THREE WEEKS prior to the beginning of the journey as approval takes up to 15 days.
- Please be reminded that processing period depends on the Embassy. If further assessment is required, the processing period could take longer than the above mentioned period of time. So please ensure that you have sufficient time to submit the visa application.
- Please note, Austria visa application center cannot accept visa application submitted less than15 days of intend travel date.
- If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same number of days /nights in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first stay.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- Please note applications can only be submitted maximum 6 months prior to the intended travel dates.
- Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
- Only Kuwaiti nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Kuwait can apply for a visa at the Application Center in Kuwait.
- Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. The Embassy of Austria in Kuwait reserves the right to invite the applicant to a personal interview.
- Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted and the visa application can only be processed upon receipt of ALL documents required by the embassy (see List of  required documents below).
- Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
- Applications for visas have to be received by The Embassy of Austria in Kuwait at least THREE WEEKS prior to the beginning of the journey as approval takes up to 15 days.
- Please be reminded that processing period depends on the Embassy. If further assessment is required, the processing period could take longer than the above mentioned period of time. So please ensure that you have sufficient time to submit the visa application.
- Please note, Austria visa application center cannot accept visa application submitted less than15 days of intend travel date.
- If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same number of days /nights in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first stay.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Business]
Please  Note:  All payments will be collected in local currency as per the local exchange rate. You may refer to the current exchange rate to arrive at an approximate amount. 
The Visa fees can be paid by Cash and Debitcard. It is  subject to change without notice. The fee is non-refundable.
The following categories of short stay applicants are exempted of Visa fees only:
Children below 6 years
 Family members of EEA member states 
 All fees are non-refundable. 

## Documents Required
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased,Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kwt/en/aut/travel-insurance)
Short stay visas (Schengen)
-Visa requirements for Kuwaiti.
-Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti
-Visa requirements for Kuwaiti. (Arabic)
-Visa requirement for Non- Kuwaiti (Arabic)
 Please note that all documents mentioned in the list are absolutely necessary for a Schengen visa. Please bring ALL the documents to the Embassy as incomplete applications cannot be processed in time and might affect your travel plans.
The short stay visa application must be submitted on a duly completed application form, which can be downloaded free of charge from this web page or can also be obtained free of charge from Austria Diplomatic Missions or Consular Offices abroad.
 When submitting the visa application, the established fee must be paid (generally). This fee is non-refundable and therefore will not be refunded if the visa application is unsuccessful. Under certain circumstances applicants may be entitled to a reduction or may be exempt from paying the fee. Please consult the Diplomatic Missions or Austria Consular Offices. For all other necessary requirements, please consult the Diplomatic Mission or Consular Office where the visa application is being made as these may vary according to the reason for the visit and the applicant's country of origin.
The maximum period for processing short stay (Schengen) visa applications is fifteen calendar days from the date the application is submitted. This period can be extended to a maximum of 30 calendar days in specific cases, especially if a more detailed examination of the application is required. By way of exception, in specific cases in which additional documentation is required, this period can be extended to a maximum of 60 calendar days. 
If the visa is issued, it must be collected in person from the competent Diplomatic Mission or Consular Office, or collected by a duly authorized representative, within a period of one month from the date that notification is given of the award. 
In addition: If traveling on business: 

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photographs (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photographs should be in color and: 
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling (without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).
- Please stick the Photograph on the Visa Application Form.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.  Photo booth meeting these requirements is available at the centre. 

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
